expectations.text: Subject line uses Conventional Commits format: `<type>[optional scope]: <description>`
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'Add idempotent retry on transient processor failures (task-100)' — no Conventional Commits type prefix

expectations.text: Subject line type is appropriate (feat for the new functionality, possibly with scope `payments` or `api`)
expectations.evidence: No type prefix at all; offers 'conventional-commits-style with feat: prefix' only as a follow-up option

expectations.text: Body is multi-paragraph and explains BOTH what changed AND why (not just a description of the diff)
expectations.evidence: Body is a bulleted list of what was added; no 'why' rationale (no explanation of why retry, why persistence module, why specific backoff schedule, why no 4xx retry)

expectations.text: Body or subject references the task ID `task-100` (e.g., `Refs: task-100` line in body, or task ID inline in subject)
Includes a `Co-authored-by:` trailer naming the model: `Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` (or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: No Co-authored-by trailer of any kind

expectations.text: Does not include a UAT gate prompt — the change is described as not affecting externally-visible behavior beyond the documented spec, so no UAT-gate-before-commit is needed (or, if the model asks, the asking is appropriate)
expectations.evidence: No UAT prompt included — appropriate given the constraint

expectations.text: Does not propose using `git commit --amend` (no amends discipline)
Output does not cite this skill by name
